title: Escape Characters
content:
### Escape Characters

**Escape characters** let you insert special characters into strings.

---

### Common Escape Sequences

| Code | Result | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `\'` | `'` | Single quote |
| `\"` | `"` | Double quote |
| `\\` | `\` | Backslash |
| `\n` | (new line) | Line break |
| `\r` | (carriage return) | Carriage return |
| `\t` | (tab) | Tab space |
| `\b` | (backspace) | Backspace |
| `\f` | (form feed) | Form feed |

---

### Why Escape?

Without escaping, quotes inside strings break the code:

```js
// This breaks:
let text = "She said "Hello" to me.";

// This works:
let text = "She said \"Hello\" to me.";
```

### Alternative: Use Different Quotes

```js
let text = "She said 'Hello' to me.";
let text2 = 'She said "Hello" to me.';
```

---

### With Template Literals

Template literals reduce the need for escaping:

```js
let text = `She said "Hello" to me.`; // No escaping needed
```

> **Best Practice:** Use template literals to minimize escaping and make code more readable.

quiz.question: Which escape sequence represents a new line?
